text:
ANATOMIC PATHOLOGY REPORT

================================================================================
PATIENT: Margaret Osei-Bonsu
MRN: [REDACTED]
DATE OF PROCEDURE: [TO BE INSERTED]
DATE OF RECEIPT: [TO BE INSERTED]
DATE OF REPORT: [TO BE INSERTED]
ACCESSION NUMBER: SP-[REDACTED]
ORDERING PHYSICIAN: [Surgical Oncology / Oncology Service]
PATHOLOGIST: [Attending Pathologist, MD]
RESIDENT/FELLOW: [Pathology Resident, MD]
================================================================================

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN SUBMITTED
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Specimen A: Left axillary lymph node excision (labeled "left axilla, excisional biopsy")

CLINICAL HISTORY: 58-year-old female with a known primary cutaneous malignant melanoma of the back (Breslow thickness 4.2 mm, ulcerated, Clark level V, 18 mm greatest dimension). Presenting with a rapidly enlarging left axillary mass clinically measuring approximately 5 cm on examination. Rule out metastatic melanoma versus primary lymph node pathology. BRAF V600E mutation status requested. PD-L1 expression requested.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Specimen A: Received fresh and subsequently fixed in 10% neutral buffered formalin, labeled with the patient's name and "left axilla, excisional biopsy," is an oriented excision of soft tissue containing one dominant lymph node. The specimen measures 5.3 x 4.2 x 3.7 cm in aggregate. External surface is bosselated and tan-pink with focal areas of adherent perinodal adipose tissue. On serial sectioning, the lymph node measures 4.8 x 3.6 x 3.1 cm and has a firm, rubbery consistency. The cut surface reveals a variegated tan-brown to slate-gray parenchyma with geographic areas of central yellow-white necrosis comprising approximately 30-40% of the nodal parenchyma. No grossly normal residual lymphoid tissue is identified. The nodal capsule appears grossly intact, though focally thinned. Perinodal soft tissue is present and submitted entirely to assess for extranodal extension. No additional discrete lymph nodes are identified in the specimen.

Representative sections submitted as follows:
  A1-A3: Lymph node, perpendicular sections including viable and necrotic areas and capsule
  A4: Lymph node, area of central necrosis
  A5: Perinodal soft tissue, entirely submitted

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Sections from the left axillary lymph node show near-complete replacement of nodal architecture by a densely cellular malignant neoplasm. The tumor is arranged in cohesive sheets and nests with focal alveolar architecture. Residual sinusoidal spaces are effaced and only rare compressed residual lymphoid follicles are identified at the periphery of the node.

The neoplastic cells are epithelioid to polygonal in shape, large, and have abundant pale eosinophilic cytoplasm with distinct cell borders. Nuclear features are pleomorphic with large vesicular nuclei, coarse chromatin, and prominent eosinophilic macronucleoli (up to 8 microns in diameter). Binucleate and multinucleate forms are present. Mitotic figures are readily identified and exceed 10 per mm2; atypical mitotic figures are noted. Focally, the cytoplasm of neoplastic cells contains coarsely granular brown-black melanin pigment, confirmed by Fontana-Masson stain (performed on select blocks). Tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes are moderate in number and are present both at the periphery of tumor nests (non-brisk pattern) and scattered within the tumor bulk.

Geographic areas of central coagulative necrosis are confirmed microscopically, involving approximately 35% of the nodal tissue. Viable tumor is identified immediately adjacent to zones of necrosis. The nodal capsule is disrupted focally with extension of tumor cells into the perinodal adipose tissue (extranodal extension present, extent: 1.2 mm beyond the capsule on one section). No lymphovascular invasion is identified in the perinodal soft tissue. The remaining perinodal adipose tissue shows reactive fibrosis and chronic inflammatory changes.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed on Specimen A, block A2 unless otherwise noted):

Panel performed to confirm melanocytic lineage and assess prognostic/predictive biomarkers:

  S100 protein (polyclonal):         POSITIVE (3+, diffuse strong nuclear and cytoplasmic staining)
  SOX10:                             POSITIVE (3+, diffuse strong nuclear staining in >95% of tumor cells)
  Melan-A (MART-1, clone A103):      POSITIVE (2-3+, granular cytoplasmic staining in approximately 80% of tumor cells)
  HMB-45 (gp100, clone HMB45):      POSITIVE (2+, patchy cytoplasmic staining in approximately 60% of tumor cells, stronger in peripheral tumor nests)
  MiTF (clone D5):                   POSITIVE (2+, nuclear, approximately 70% of tumor cells)
  Pankeratin (AE1/AE3):              NEGATIVE (no staining in tumor cells; positive internal control present)
  CD45 (LCA):                        NEGATIVE (tumor cells; residual lymphocytes positive as internal control)
  CD30:                              NEGATIVE
  Ki-67 (MIB-1):                     Approximately 45% nuclear positivity (high proliferative index)

  BRAF V600E mutation-specific immunohistochemistry (VE1 antibody, Spring Bioscience):
                                     STRONGLY POSITIVE (3+, diffuse strong cytoplasmic granular staining in >95% of viable tumor cells). This result is consistent with the presence of a BRAF p.V600E mutation. Note: IHC is a surrogate marker; molecular confirmation is recommended and has been requested separately.

  PD-L1 (22C3 pharmDx, Dako, KEYTRUDA companion diagnostic platform):
                                     POSITIVE
                                     Tumor Proportion Score (TPS): 30%
                                       (TPS: proportion of viable tumor cells with partial or complete membrane staining at any intensity)
                                     Combined Positive Score (CPS): 55
                                       (CPS: number of PD-L1-staining cells [tumor cells + lymphocytes + macrophages] divided by total viable tumor cells x 100)
                                     Interpretation: TPS >= 1%; CPS >= 1. PD-L1 expression present. Scoring performed per manufacturer's instructions by a trained PD-L1 scoring pathologist.

Fontana-Masson stain: Positive for melanin pigment within the cytoplasm of a subset of neoplastic cells (approximately 20-25% of cells), confirming melanin production.

PAS (Periodic acid-Schiff): Negative for intracytoplasmic glycogen or mucin.

MOLECULAR / GENOMIC STUDIES (PENDING AT TIME OF PRELIMINARY REPORT):
  - BRAF mutation analysis by next-generation sequencing or allele-specific PCR: PENDING (sent to [Molecular Pathology Laboratory]). Result to be reported as addendum.
  - Comprehensive tumor profiling (solid tumor NGS panel, [institutional/external laboratory]): PENDING. Result to be reported as addendum.
  - Additional biomarker studies as clinically indicated: PENDING.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSIS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specimen A: LEFT AXILLARY LYMPH NODE, EXCISIONAL BIOPSY:

  METASTATIC MALIGNANT MELANOMA

  -- Morphology: Epithelioid melanoma, with frequent mitoses (>10/mm2) and areas of geographic coagulative necrosis
  -- Tumor size within lymph node: 4.8 cm (greatest dimension)
  -- Extranodal extension: PRESENT (1.2 mm beyond capsule)
  -- Melanin pigment: Present focally (confirmed by Fontana-Masson)
  -- Residual nodal architecture: Extensively effaced; only rare peripheral lymphoid remnants identified
  -- Lymphovascular invasion (perinodal): Not identified
  -- Margins of excision: Perinodal soft tissue margin involved by extranodal extension (tumor present at soft tissue margin)

  Immunohistochemical profile is consistent with melanocytic lineage (S100+, SOX10+, Melan-A+, HMB-45+, MiTF+, pankeratin-, CD45-).

  BRAF V600E-SPECIFIC IHC (VE1): Strongly positive (3+). Findings are consistent with BRAF V600E mutation. Confirmatory molecular testing pending (see above).

  PD-L1 EXPRESSION (22C3 pharmDx): TPS 30%, CPS 55. See ancillary studies section for details.

  Ki-67 PROLIFERATIVE INDEX: Approximately 45%.

  Clinical history is consistent with a cutaneous primary site (known primary on the back). The immunophenotype and morphology support metastatic malignant melanoma consistent with cutaneous primary origin. Uveal or mucosal primary sites are considered less likely given the clinical context; however, no immunohistochemical marker definitively excludes these.

  NOTE: Molecular profiling results are pending and will be reported as an addendum. Correlation with clinical and radiographic findings is recommended.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SIGNED BY: [Attending Pathologist, MD, FCAP]
[Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine]
[Institution Name]

Case reviewed with [Pathology Resident, MD].
Case discussed at [Multidisciplinary Melanoma Tumor Board, date pending].

Preliminary report released electronically; final report with addendum pending molecular testing results.
================================================================================
END OF REPORT
================================================================================
